data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: AAPL, GME, STLA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures continued to edge lower on Friday as fresh retaliatory tariffs from China intensified concerns that the escalating trade conflict could trigger a global recession, following Wall Street’s sharpest sell-off in five years, driven by President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariff plan.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • GameStop (NYSE:GME) +3% – Shares gained after a regulatory filing revealed CEO Ryan Cohen purchased 500,000 shares in the open market at $21.55, raising his stake to 37.3M shares, or about 8.4% of the company, signaling renewed confidence in the video game retailer. The stock had fallen 7% on Thursday amid a broader sell-off triggered by Trump’s tariff announcement. GameStop recently raised $1.3B through a convertible note offering due in 2030, with plans to use the proceeds to purchase bitcoin.
  • MarketAxess (NASDAQ:MKTX) +5% – Shares rose after the company posted record trading volumes for March and Q1 2025. March total ADV hit $46.5B (+46% Y/Y), and Q1 ADV reached $42.9B (+31%). Credit ADV was $17.8B in March and $15.9B for the quarter. U.S. high-grade ADV hit $8.7B in March, while emerging markets and Eurobonds contributed $3.9B and $2.3B in Q1, respectively. Portfolio trading surged 134% Y/Y to $1.5B in March. Open Trading ADV was $5.5B, making up 37% of total credit volume.

Biggest stock losers

  • Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) -3% – Shares declined after the automaker announced temporary layoffs of 900 workers at five U.S. facilities and paused production at assembly plants in Mexico and Canada in response to President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariff measures. The new policy, which imposes a baseline 10% tariff on all imports and a prior 25% duty on auto imports, has rattled the global auto sector. In a letter to employees, Americas COO Antonio Filosa said the company is “continuing to assess the medium- and long-term effects” of the tariffs but has opted to take immediate action, including halting operations at select sites.
  • BP (NYSE:BP) -3% – Shares slipped after the company announced Chairman Helge Lund plans to step down in 2026. A succession process is underway, led by senior independent director Dame Amanda Blanc and the wider board, to identify his replacement. The incoming chair will join BP’s board ahead of the transition to ensure continuity. Lund said the timing is right to begin the search, emphasizing the company's sharpened focus on strategy execution, performance, and shareholder value. He has backed BP’s 2020 pivot from oil and gas to broader energy initiatives aimed at enhancing profitability.
  • Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) -5% – Shares extended losses after China announced a retaliatory 34% tariff on U.S. goods, effective April 10. This adds to Apple’s 9% drop on Thursday, as fears mount over the company's heavy reliance on Chinese manufacturing. China accounts for roughly 80% of Apple’s production capacity, with an estimated 90% of iPhones assembled there, per Evercore ISI. Beijing slammed the U.S. for raising its total tariffs on China to 54%, calling the move a violation of international trade norms. Separately, Needham warned that Apple’s full-year fiscal 2025 EPS could plunge 28% to $5.27 from $7.32 if the company does not receive an exemption from President Donald Trump’s "Liberation Day" tariff plan.
  • DuPont (NYSE:DD) -13% – Shares sank after China launched an anti-monopoly investigation into DuPont China over suspected violations and amid concerns that new tariffs could significantly impact the company’s operations.
  • NIO (NYSE:NIO) -9% – Shares slid after the EV maker reportedly cut jobs across its European R&D teams to slash operational costs by 25%, according to an internal memo. The move follows recent layoffs in the U.S. and China. NIO’s Q1 sales fell sharply in Germany, the Netherlands, and Norway, with Sweden the only market showing growth. The broader drop in Chinese stocks followed China’s 34% tariff retaliation. XPeng (XPEV), JD.com (JD) -9%, Li Auto (LI) -7%.

data.title: Biogen downgraded to Hold at Argus on profit decline expectations
data.description:
• Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) was downgraded to Hold from Buy at Argus, citing expected decline for profit per share in 2025.
  • In February, Biogen said that it expects 2025 non-GAAP EPS of $15.25-$16.25 and a mid-single-digit decline in total revenue due to multiple sclerosis franchise pressures, including potential biosimilar and generic entries.
  • "While the company has been at the forefront of the development of treatments for Alzheimer's disease (AD), with its drug Leqembi (lecanemab-irmb) now approved for the treatment of early Alzheimer's disease in a number of key markets, it has also experienced setbacks, with other countries denying their approvals for Leqembi due to safety concerns," Argus analyst Jasper Hellweg said in a report.
  • Analyst also sees the multiple sclerosis business' revenue decline as a factor for the downgrade.
  • Biogen is down 3% in morning trade.

data.title: Nio cuts European jobs to lower operational costs by 25%
data.description:
• Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer NIO Inc (NYSE:NIO) has reportedly conducted another round of layoffs in Europe with the aim of cutting its operational costs by 25%, according to internal memos viewed by industry publication EV.
  • The cuts were primarily in the company’s research & development teams.
  • In the last quarter, NIO’s (NYSE:NIO) European sales were down by as much as double-digits in Germany, the Netherlands, and Norway, with Sweden the only country showing an improvement in sales.
  • The latest job cuts follow similar reductions across its operations in the U.S. and China across several departments, including its smart driving delivery teams, and end-to-end technology teams.
  • NIO (NYSE:NIO) shares are under heavy selling pressure in tandem with U.S.-listed Chinese stocks after Beijing retaliated to President Trump’s sweeping tariffs with a 34% tariff on U.S. imports.

data.title: Lattice initiated with Buy rating due to growth in specialty chip market: Loop
data.description:
Lattice Semiconductor (NASDAQ:LSCC) received a Buy rating as Loop Capital initiated coverage, due to its ability to grow market share.

Loop expects LSCC's expansion into the field programmable gate array (FPGA) will prompt high-teens percentage compound annual growth. This is due to FPGAs becoming a viable option to the more expensive and less flexible application specific integrate circuit, or ASIC, and application specific standard product (ASSP) alternatives.

Loop also assigned an $85 price target on the stock.

Headquartered in Hillsboro, Ore., Lattice produces FPGAs and related semiconductor products for an array of applications, including communications, computing, industrial, automotive and consumer markets. Lattice shares took a beating, along with the rest of the tech sector, on Thursday, plummeting 15%, due to tariff concerns. It was down another 4% during Friday morning market action.

"We see three main drivers for 70%-plus and 40%-plus gross and operating margins, respectively, for LSCC (vs. 67%/25% in FY24)," said Loop analyst Gary Mobley, in a Friday investor note. "First, mix tailwinds will help (e.g., new product mix, software, end-market mix, etc.). Second, we see relatively good pricing power in the FPGA market, limited competition and a tailwind from newer products like Avant (as much as 20.0x ASP boost). Last, the move to lower cost regions like India & China for R&D should continue to act as a structural tailwind to LSCC's R&D expenses."

Two companies, Xilinx and Altera, historically dominated the FPGA market, accounting for about 80%. However, those companies have since been acquired by Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC), respectively.

"Now that Altera and Xilinx have been absorbed by processor companies mostly focused on a systems approach to data center compute, this has left the door open for companies like LSCC to absorb a growing void in the low-and mid-tier portion of the FPGA market," Mobley said. 

"Additionally, there is uncertainty swirling around Intel's strategic direction, including to whom Altera gets sold to," he added. "Related, the future of Altera's newest products rely heavily on the future success of Intel Foundry. We believe LSCC can capitalize on this uncertainty."

data.title: American Battery to sell legacy property in Fernley, Nevada for $6.75M
data.description:
• American Battery Technology (NASDAQ:ABAT) has announced the sale of one of its unused legacy properties, located at 395 Logan Lane in Fernley, Nevada.
  • This 12-acre property entered into contract to sell for $6.75M, with the transaction expected to close on or before July 10.
  • This strategic sale will support the company's mission to advance its lithium-ion battery recycling technologies and streamlined operations at its commercial-scale battery recycling facility in the Tahoe-Reno Industrial Center at 2500 Peru Drive, McCarran, Nevada.
  • The prioritization of this operational facility at TRIC allows ABTC to continue its focused implementation on its recycling processes that are critical to developing a domestic supply of refined battery materials.
  • The company continues to evaluate opportunities for its real property asset at 390 Logan Lane and the company's water rights holdings located in Fernley, Nevada.

data.title: Two new bitcoin strategy ETFs launched
data.description:
• ETF provider and asset manager First Trust Advisors announced on Friday the launch of two new bitcoin strategy ETFs.
  • The new launches are the Bitcoin Strategy Floor15 ETF Series of Target Outcome ETFs®: the FT Vest Bitcoin Strategy Floor15 ETF - April (NYSEARCA:BFAP) and the FT Vest Bitcoin Strategy & Target Income ETF (NYSEARCA:DFII).

data.title: Alaska Air takes a stake in VR flight simulation firm Loft Dynamics
data.description:
Alaska Air Group(NYSE:ALK) announced a strategic investment in Loft Dynamics to develop the first hyper-realistic, full-motion Boeing 737 virtual reality simulator. The airline company made the investment through Alaska Star Ventures.

Loft Dynamics is described as a Swiss-based company specializing in virtual reality flight simulation technology for pilot training. Founded in 2016 by Fabi Riesen, Loft Dynamics is headquartered in Dübendorf, Zürich, Switzerland, with a North American office in Santa Monica, California.

The goal of Alaska Air's (NYSE:ALK) investment is to revolutionize pilot training by leveraging extended reality technology. Loft Dynamics is recognized for its FAA- and EASA-qualified VR helicopter simulators, and the partnership marks its expansion into fixed-wing aircraft training. 

The VR simulators promise to enhance pilot training efficiency and safety while addressing challenges such as the global pilot shortage. The Boeing 737 VR simulator will feature cutting-edge technologies, including six-degrees-of-freedom motion platforms, 360-degree panoramic 3D views, advanced pose tracking, and customizable scenarios. Compact in design, these simulators require significantly less space than traditional full-motion devices, making them more accessible and scalable. 

Alaska Air (ALK) plans to install the simulators at its bases once they receive FAA approval. Looking ahead, the company pointed to the potential of VR technology to transform pilot training by offering immersive, data-driven experiences that replicate real-world flight conditions. The innovation aligns with the company's history of pioneering aviation advancements.

Shares of Alaska Air (ALK) were down 4.4% in early trading on Friday amid broad market declines.

data.title: Trump assures investors that his policies 'never change'
data.description:
President Donald Trump sought to assure investors on Friday morning that the United States is still a good place to invest.

"TO THE MANY INVESTORS COMING INTO THE UNITED STATES AND INVESTING MASSIVE AMOUNTS OF MONEY, MY POLICIES WILL NEVER CHANGE. THIS IS A GREAT TIME TO GET RICH, RICHER THAN EVER BEFORE!!!" he wrote on a social media post.

Still, equity markets continue to bleed red, with the Nasdaq dropping 4.3%, S&P 500 falling 3.2%, and the Dow off 2.8% in early Friday trading. The 10-year Treasury yield sank below the 4% mark to 3.94%. The Dollar Index, meanwhile, edged up 0.1%.

He also touted the March jobs numbers, which came in stronger than expected, adding that his policies are already working. "HANG TOUGH, WE CAN’T LOSE!!!," he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: PayPal adds Chainlink, Solana cryptocurrencies to its platforms
data.description:
PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL) (NEOE:PYPL:CA) announced on Friday the addition of two cryptocurrencies - Chainlink (LINK-USD) and Solana (SOL-USD) - to its PayPal and Venmo platforms.

Shares were trading -3.73% pre-market at $59.41.

PayPal and Venmo users can buy, hold, sell, and transfer the two cryptocurrencies directly in their respective accounts.

The announcement comes as cryptocurrency-linked stocks face downward pressure on the back of President Donald Trump's new import tariffs.

"Since we initially made cryptocurrencies available on PayPal and Venmo, we've been listening to our users about what they want to do with crypto on our platforms. One piece of feedback we've heard is to make additional tokens available that align with our mission of revolutionizing payments," said May Zabaneh, vice president of product, blockchain, crypto, and digital currencies, at the digital payments company.

"Offering more tokens on PayPal and Venmo provides users with greater flexibility, choice, and access to digital currencies. This allows for more options to buy, send or spend within our trusted PayPal and Venmo wallets," added Zabaneh.

data.title: Meta likely to release Llama 4 model later this month: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) is likely to release the next large language model in the Llama model family, Llama 4, later this month, The Information reported.

The release of Llama 4 has been pushed back at least twice due to performance issues, with underperformance in reasoning and math tasks, the news outlet added, citing two people familiar with the matter. There are also concerns that Llama 4 is less capable than OpenAI's models in having humanlike voice conversations, the people added.

Meta unveiled its most recent Llama model, Llama 3.3, in December. 

Several of Meta's competitors have released new versions of their large language models in recent weeks, including Google (GOOGL) (GOOGL), which released Gemini 2.5 on March 25. 

Meta is also discussing offering a Llama API for businesses as part of an internal program known, Llama X. This could let companies use Llama more easily in their own software and provide Meta with added revenue, the news outlet added, citing three people familiar with the matter.

Morgan Stanley said in March that the release of Llama 4 could come in the Spring of 2025. The company is also gearing up for its LlamaCon developer event next week, where it is expected to provide updates on the Llama family of models, along with other AI innovations,

Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg said in October 2024 that the company's upcoming models are “being trained on a cluster that is bigger than 100,000 [Nvidia] H100s or bigger than anything that I've seen reported for what others are doing.”

Last month, Meta announced that its Llama artificial intelligence models have been downloaded more than 1B times.
